---
title: SENTRY Surveillance
alias: &ALIAS sentry
href: https://www.jmilabs.com/sentry-surveillance-program/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The SENTRY Antimicrobial Surveillance Program is a multinational longitudinal surveillance network monitoring global antimicrobial resistance trends and pathogen prevalence in clinical isolates.

## Key Features

- Continuously active since 1997, providing over two decades of longitudinal AMR trend data
- Covers isolates from blood, urine, respiratory, and wound infection sites across global regions
- Monitors susceptibility to a wide panel of current and investigational antimicrobial agents
- Publishes annual and regional reports in peer-reviewed journals with standardized methodology
- Provides MIC distribution data enabling epidemiological cutoff analyses

## Use Cases in Research

- Tracking long-term global trends in resistance rates for major clinical pathogens
- Benchmarking in vitro activity of novel antimicrobial candidates against contemporary clinical isolates
- Generating AMR epidemiological priors for Bayesian or machine learning resistance prediction models
- Comparing SENTRY data with in-house clinical microbiology data for local vs. global AMR context
- Informing empiric antibiotic prescribing guidelines using region-specific prevalence data

## Getting Started

Visit [SENTRY Surveillance](https://www.jmilabs.com/sentry-surveillance-program/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
